title: Anisotome gingidium
taxonomy: Anisotome gingidium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, and is native to New Zealand. It belongs to the genus Anisotome, which is a small group of plants endemic to the country. The specific epithet 'gingidium' is derived from the Greek word 'gingidion', meaning 'little ginger', likely referring to its aromatic properties. Anisotome gingidium is a perennial herb with fern-like leaves and produces white or yellowish flowers. This plant is often found growing in wetlands, along streams, and in shaded areas of the New Zealand countryside.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from digestive issues, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties.
